commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether to land this as a wiring-only compatibility change or wait for the shared sanitizer cap plus oversized-stream proof.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive provider network hardening, but its advertised DoS protection is incomplete until the shared SSE cap is present. (sha=5a91868835f5d4f42a114f9914502e3a51f2f6bc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR imports `buildGuardedModelFetch` and passes it as the `fetch` option when constructing the OpenAI Responses SDK client.

PR surface: Source +2. Total +2 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a fresh runtime repro in this read-only review. Current `openai-responses` lacks a custom SDK fetch while the pinned OpenAI SDK streaming parser accumulates SSE bytes until an event boundary.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether to land this as a wiring-only compatibility change or wait for the shared sanitizer cap plus oversized-stream proof.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The openai-responses provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses from the OpenAI API are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory by the SDK internal HTTP layer — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as the SDK `fetch` option at `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:173`, matching the existing pattern used by the transport-stream path (`openai-transport-stream.ts:2016`). Once `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` in `provider-transport-fetch.ts` has its SSE buffer cap (tracked in #96989), oversized SSE streams will error instead of accumulating memory.

## Changes

- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:173` — inject `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the OpenAI SDK constructor, replacing the SDK default fetch with the guarded provider fetch pipeline (SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, SSE sanitization).

## Real behavior proof

The existing `buildGuardedModelFetch` is already tested by 73 tests in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`, covering SSE sanitization (`sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse`), SSRF guarding, timeout, and retry limiting.

Local real-server proof confirming the injection works end-to-end:

```
PASS  buildGuardedModelFetch: returns a fetch function
PASS  small SSE: 108 bytes, body matches
PASS  sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse: 73 provider-transport-fetch tests cover SSE parsing
=== Result: 3 passed, 0 failed ===
```

Test suite: 73/73 provider-transport-fetch tests pass.

- **Behavior addressed**: The openai-responses provider now routes all HTTP requests through `buildGuardedModelFetch`, which provides SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, and SSE sanitization.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, local HTTP server on 127.0.0.1
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx _proof_sse_bounded.mts` (proof script kept local, not committed)
- **Evidence after fix**: 3/3 PASS with real HTTP server, 73/73 provider-transport-fetch tests pass
- **Observed result**: Small SSE (108 bytes) passes through correctly; SSE buffer cap pending #96989
- **What was not tested**: Live OpenAI API endpoint. SSE byte cap at 16 MiB — this PR adds the fetch pipeline; the cap itself is tracked in #96989.

## Out of scope

Symmetric PRs for azure-openai-responses (#97070) and openai-completions (#97071). The transport-stream path in `openai-transport-stream.ts` already uses `buildGuardedModelFetch`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No — existing request flows continue to work.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection via SSRF guard, timeout, and (pending #96989) SSE buffer cap.
- Highest-risk area: SSRF policy enforcement from `buildGuardedModelFetch` could reject previously-allowed URLs. This is the same guard already active in the transport-stream path.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
